'''Adam Quinlan''' (born 13 November 1992) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|fb}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] in two separate spells in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Quinlan was born in [[Kogarah]], [[New South Wales]], Australia.

==Playing career==
===St. George Illawarra (2013–14)===
Quinlan played his junior [[rugby league]] for the Culburra Dolphins, before being signed by [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]].

In 2012, Quinlan played for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]]' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|National Youth Competition]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/q/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150702175031/http://nycdatabase.org/players/q/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=2 July 2015|title=Q|date=27 December 2012|publisher=Nyc Database|accessdate=2015-07-10}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://rleague.com/players/adam-quinlan_3740/Stats/|accessdate=4 February 2016|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130812033426/http://rleague.com/players/adam-quinlan_3740/Stats/|archivedate=12 August 2013|title=Adam Quinlan: Stats}}</ref>
<!-- 
[[File:Adamquinlan.jpg|thumb|right|Quinlan playing for the Illawarra Cutters in 2013]]
 -->

In 2013, Quinlan graduated onto the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]] [[New South Wales Cup]] team, the [[Illawarra Cutters]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dragons.com.au/news/2013/03/26/illawarra-cutters-team-list-round-4.html|title=Illawarra Cutters Team List: Round 4|publisher=Dragons.com.au|accessdate=2015-07-10|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150703103729/http://www.dragons.com.au/news/2013/03/26/illawarra-cutters-team-list-round-4.html|archive-date=3 July 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In round 9 of the [[2013 NRL season]], he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] début for [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George]] against the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]] playing at {{rlp|fb}} in club's 15–14 loss, at [[Robina Stadium|Cbus Super Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dragons.com.au/news-display/Late-Mail-Round-9/74461|accessdate=4 February 2016|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131202234930/http://www.dragons.com.au/news-display/Late-Mail-Round-9/74461|archivedate=2 December 2013|title=Late Mail: Round 9 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/gold-coast-titans-field-goal-seals-one-point-thriller-against-st-george-illawarra-dragons/story-fn2mcuj6-1226640583881?nk=384b5f4da26cb41434e517c9fd789e7b|title=Gold Coast Titans field goal seals one-point thriller against St George Illawarra Dragons|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|accessdate=2015-07-10}}</ref>

In only his third match in round 19, he scored his first [[NRL]] career try in the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George]]'s 22–18 win, over [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] at [[Stadium Australia|ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/live-nrl-south-sydney-rabbitohs-v-st-george-illawarra-dragons/story-fni3gki8-1226683337916|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs v St George Illwarra Dragons|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|accessdate=2015-07-10}}</ref>

On 31 July 2013, he re-signed with [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George]] on a two-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/quinlan-re-signs-with-dragons-10603/|title=Quinlan re-signs with Dragons | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2013-07-31|accessdate=2015-07-10|archive-date=15 March 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140315172259/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/quinlan-re-signs-with-dragons-10603/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

He finished off his début year in the [[NRL]] having played in 10 matches and scoring 5 tries in total.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/OS0xNjI5LTQtLS0tLS0yMDIyMC0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLQ==|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-02-02|accessdate=2015-07-10}}</ref>

In February 2014, Quinlan played for [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George]] in the inaugural [[2014 NRL Auckland Nines|NRL Auckland Nines]]. He finished off the [[2014 NRL season|2014 season]] having played in 15 matches and scoring three tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=Squads For Auckland Nines|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2014-02-14|accessdate=2015-07-10|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archive-date=15 January 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/OS0xNjQ0LTQtLS0tLS0yMDIyMC0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLQ==|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-02-03|accessdate=2015-07-10}}</ref>

===Parramatta Eels (2015)===
On 27 January 2015, Quinlan signed a one-year contract with the [[Parramatta Eels]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2015/01/27/quinlan_joins_parram.html|title=Quinlan Joins Parramatta Eels|publisher=Parraeels.com.au|accessdate=2015-07-10}}</ref>

Quinlan played for the [[Wentworthville Magpies]], [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]]'s feeder club in the [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW|Intrust Super Premiership]].<ref name="Adam Quinlan joins St Helens from Parramatta amid full-back crisis">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/jul/01/adam-quinlan-st-helens-parramatta-full-back-crisis|title=Adam Quinlan joins St Helens from Parramatta amid full-back crisis|work=The Guardian|date=1 July 2015|accessdate=14 February 2017}}</ref>
[[File:adamquinlan2015.jpg|thumb|upright|Quinlan playing for the [[Wentworthville Magpies]] in 2015]]

===St Helens (2015)===
On 2 July 2015, without playing a first-grade game for the [[Parramatta Eels]]. He joined [[Super League]] club [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. The move came mid-season effective immediately, after being released from his [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sbs.com.au/news/article/2015/07/02/parras-quinlan-signs-super-league|title=Parra's Quinlan signs with Super League | SBS News|publisher=Sbs.com.au|date=2015-07-02|accessdate=2015-07-10}}</ref>

Quinlan made 12 appearances and scored 6 tries for the [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], in a short-spell at the club.

===St. George Illawarra Dragons (2016)===
In 2016, Quinlan returned to [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]] on a one-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/quinlan-returns-to-dragons-19862/#xyrFy00qiUK4X9V1.97|title=Quinlan returns to Dragons | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2014-07-27|accessdate=2015-12-01|archive-date=2 December 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151202220146/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/quinlan-returns-to-dragons-19862/#xyrFy00qiUK4X9V1.97|url-status=dead}}</ref>
[[File:Adam Quinlan Illawarra.jpg|thumb|upright|Quinlan playing for Illawarra in 2016]]
Quinlan was a member of the [[Illawarra Cutters]] side which won the 2016 [[New South Wales Cup]], with a subsequent 21–20 victory over the [[Mount Pritchard Mounties]] in the final.<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2016/09/25/cutters_win_the_gran.html| title = Cutters Win The Grand Final - NSWRL}}</ref>

Quinlan then went onto play in the State Championship match at [[Stadium Australia]] against [[Queensland Cup]] winners the [[Burleigh Bears]], with the [[Illawarra Cutters]] winning the match 54–12, with Quinlan scoring a try.<ref>{{cite web| url = http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/qrl/state-championship-2016-burleigh-bears-fairytale-season-over-as-illawarra-cutters-run-riot-5412/news-story/274b57afa075c164dd7cd581d81febcb| title = The Courier Mail }}{{Dead link|date=September 2023}}{{cbignore}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers (2017 – 2021)===
Quinlan joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] on a two-year contract, prior to the start of the 2017 season. Quinlan earned promotion with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] back to the [[Super League]] in his first season with the club.<ref>{{cite web|author=Zero Tackle|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/hull-kr-sign-fullback-adam-quinlan-24797/|title=Hull KR sign fullback Adam Quinlan|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2016-12-22|accessdate=2017-02-14}}</ref>
It was revealed on 4 July 2018, that Quinlan had signed a two-year contract extension, to extend his stay beyond the 2018 [[rugby league]] season and to remain at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] until at least the end of the 2020 campaign.<ref>{{cite web|date=4 July 2018|title=Quinlan signs contract extension|url=http://mobile.twitter.com/hullkrofficial/status/1014577081613774859}}</ref>

On 9 September 2018, during a 38–24 [[The Qualifiers|Qualifiers]] triumph during a game against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], Quinlan unfortunately suffered a season ending [[anterior cruciate ligament]] (ACL) knee injury that required corrective surgery and that would prove to rule him out of action for the foreseeable future.

In December 2021 Adam Quinlan announced his retirement from rugby league<ref>{{cite web|date=9 December 2021|title=Hull KR full-back retires|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/tony-smith-adam-quinlan-claim-6328236}}</ref> On 7 December 2021, Quinlan confirmed that he would retire after he failed to secure another contract with an English club, following his exit from [[Hull KR]].<ref>{{cite web|website=St Helens Reporter|date=7 Dec 2021|accessdate=1 Jan 2022|title=Former St Helens and Hull KR full-back Adam Quinlan has decided to call time on his playing career at the age of 29|url=https://www.sthelensreporter.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/former-st-helens-full-back-adam-quinlan-calls-time-on-playing-career-at-29-3485291 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211214221426/https://www.sthelensreporter.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/former-st-helens-full-back-adam-quinlan-calls-time-on-playing-career-at-29-3485291 |archive-date=14 December 2021}}</ref>

==Honours==
===Career Awards and Accolades===
===Club (Hull Kingston Rovers 2017 – present)===

* 2018: [[Roger Millward]] – 'Player of the Year Award'
* 2018: 'Players' Player of the Year Award'
* 2018: 'Members' Player of the Year Award'
* 2018: 'Tackle of the Season Award'<ref>{{cite web|date=4 October 2018|title=Quinlan dominates 2018 Player of the Year awards|url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/quinlan-dominates-2018-player-of-the-year-awards/}}</ref>
